sql: select 'vehicles' as table_name, count(*) from dune.vehicles union all select 'vehicle_modules', count(*) from dune.vehicle_modules union all select 'backup_vehicles', count(*) from dune.backup_vehicles union all select 'recovered_vehicles', count(*) from dune.recovered_vehicles;

select p.proname, pg_get_function_identity_arguments(p.oid) as args, pg_get_function_result(p.oid) as result from pg_proc p join pg_namespace n on n.oid=p.pronamespace where n.nspname='dune' and p.proname ilike '%vehicle%' order by p.proname;

operatorSteps: Record the test racers and run: python3 scripts/smugglers-run-mp.py --env-file .env init --entrant <name1> --entrant <name2> --vehicle-mode both
Have each player bring an owned sandbike to Smugglers Run and drop/deploy it inside the map.
Run a short race, exit the map, relog, and verify each player still owns and can recover/use the same bike.
If owned bikes fail, repeat with GM-spawned loaner bikes and leave player-owned bikes outside the map.

sql: select * from dune.vehicles order by id desc limit 25;
select * from dune.vehicle_modules order by id desc limit 100;
select * from dune.backup_vehicles order by account_id desc nulls last limit 25;
select * from dune.recovered_vehicles order by account_id desc nulls last limit 25;
select account_id, "group", locator_name, map, dimension, last_used_timestamp from dune.player_respawn_locations where "group"='Vehicle' order by last_used_timestamp desc nulls last limit 50;

pass: Owned vehicle actor/module rows remain associated with the same player/account.
No unexpected recovered_vehicles or backup_vehicles row appears for a racer after normal exit.
Vehicle respawn/recovery location remains usable by the owner.
fail: Bike becomes ownerless, moves to recovered/backup state unexpectedly, loses modules/inventory, or cannot be used by the owner after relog.
Dropping a bike inside the map changes ownership or deletes module inventory rows.
